extraction.one_line_summary: EWAN Caleb timed his sprint to perfection, jumping ACKERMANN Pascal's train early then waiting until 450m to launch, beating DÉMARE Arnaud on the line at Novi Ligure.
tactical_patterns: EWAN deliberately chose ACKERMANN's wheel despite having own train, revealing leadout quality matters more than team loyalty in crosswinds
CIMA Damiano aggressively accumulated intermediate sprint points from breakaway, suggesting targeted classification hunting rather than stage hunting
Bora-Hansgrohe deployed five riders mid-pack with 25km remaining, excessive positioning investment that failed to prevent EWAN's wheel-jump
GUARNIERI Jacopo delivered VIVIANI too far back at 1km, dimension data's leadout timing consistently 200-300m earlier than optimal

rider_signals.EWAN Caleb: positive: Relaxation after first win improved energy conservation; tactical maturity shown by abandoning own train for superior positioning
rider_signals.ACKERMANN Pascal: negative: Launched 450m out in crosswind created perfect slipstream for wheel-surfers; train timing vulnerability exposed twice
rider_signals.VIVIANI Elia: negative: Fourth stage without win despite Italian champion jersey; positional discipline failures suggest leadout communication breakdown
rider_signals.DÉMARE Arnaud: positive: Consistent podium finishes without dedicated train reveal exceptional positioning instincts in chaotic finales
rider_signals.CIMA Damiano: positive: Over 700km breakaway distance plus intermediate sprint targeting indicates clear secondary classification strategy, not desperation
rider_signals.FRAPPORTI Marco: neutral: 800km breakaway accumulation suggests team directive prioritizing TV exposure over stage results
rider_signals.CONTI Valerio: neutral: Retained maglia rosa without defensive effort, confirming peloton's consensus on temporary GC situation
